Please supply the full personnel files of the following individuals: 

Sharnette Sims, Principal at York High School 

This includes any resumes, applications, any job recommendations, promotions, professional license credentials and verification records. Please include a work/salary history. Please include any investigative materials - investigative memorandums and interview summary reports, disciplinary actions (suspensions, leaves of absence that stem from disciplinary action), incident reports, EOCO investigations, Office of Civil Rights determinations, non-disciplinary memorandum of understandings, non-union misconduct form, full warning resolutions and any complaints or claims/lawsuits/settlements. Please include any safety plans. Please include any separation agreements and any other disclosure agreements. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2247077/ 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please be advised that certain private information (personal descriptions, employee ID numbers, college transcripts, social security numbers, signatures, personal addresses, health information, etc.) was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a) exempts “ information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State Law … ” (5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a)) Specifically, 105 ILCS 5/10-21.9 (b) states that “ any information concerning the record of convictions obtained by the president of the school board or the regional superintendent shall be confidential and may only be transmitted to the superintendent of the school district or his designee, the appropriate regional superintendent if the check was requested by the school district, the presidents of the appropriate school boards if the check was requested from the Department of State Police by the regional superintendent, the State Superintendent of Education, the State Teacher Certification Board, any other person necessary to the decision of hiring the applicant for employment, or for clarification purposes the Department of State Police or Statewide Sex Offender Database, or both.” 
Portions of this request that include personnel evaluations are redacted entirely in accordance with section 7(1)(a) of FOIA (5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a)) which exempts from public release information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or State law and also in accordance with section 7.5(q) of the FOIA (5 ILCS 140/7.5(q)) which exempts from public release information prohibited from being disclosed by the Personnel Records Review Act [820 ILCS 40/0.01 et seq.] Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). 
Redactions have been made pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r), which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA prohibits the disclosure of students and any student records in which a student may be individually identified by a school regardless of how or where the information pursuant to (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Redactions have also been made in accordance with 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(j)(iii) which exempts from release information concerning a school or university’s adjudication of student disciplinary cases, but only to the extent that disclosure would unavoidably reveal the identity of the student. Names and personal information of complainants and victims have been redacted pursuant to section 7(d)(iv) of the FOIA which exempts confidential information furnished only by the confidential source, or persons who file complaints with or provide information to administrative, investigative, law enforcement, or penal agencies. You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

State of Delaware 2025 compensation data for all employees.
Please provide the following fields for each employee in the dataset:

Employee # | Last Name | First Name | Middle Name/Initial | Race | Gender | Agency | Department | Job Title | Compensation (base pay, hourly rate, total pay, etc.) | Overtime | Benefits Pay | Date Hired | Date Terminated | Full-time/Part-time | Location of Work City/State

Note: If not all fields listed above are releasable as public record or available please disregard those individual fields for this request.
I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by either e-mail attachment or FTP Download if available, otherwise sent through mail via CD/DVD. I would appreciate the request to be filled in an accessible format, including but not limited to: CSV, Excel, Microsoft Access database, or other bulk list file type.
The requested data will be made available to the public free of charge and will not be used for commercial solicitation purposes. In the event that fees for personnel time and materials can not be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Tyler King.

FOIA Coordinator, OMB

communications.subject: RE: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: State of Delaware Salaries 2025
communications.communication:
Good morning, 

As stated previously, there is not a section on the FOIA form to manually enter information confirming citizenship, as requested. There is a section to provide a preferred mailing address. As you know, hopefully, an individual may have a separate preferred mailing address than their residential address. 

Aside from manually entering the residential information in an email reply to the denial based on residency (which is not equivalent to preferred mailing address), what other "manual entry" options are present on the FOIA form present on the State's website? I eagerly await your response.

title: Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) Records Request (South San Francisco Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) Records Request (South San Francisco Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

We request the following records related to Automated License Plate Readers ("ALPRs") and your law enforcement agency ("Agency").
Part A - Information related to ALPR data sharing.
A1. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shared ALPR data in 2025;
A2. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency received ALPR data in 2025;
A3. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information in 2025;
A4. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information in 2025.
The information requested in items A1-A4 is readily available for extraction through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Data Sharing Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function.
Most agencies have encountered little difficulty in providing this information (see below). In a small number of cases, an agency has interpreted items A1-A4 as requesting the creation of a record that does not exist. We disagree: this data is maintained by your software, can be exported through software your agency has already purchased, and is therefore subject to CPRA.
However, should you disagree, you may alternatively produce the following records:
A5. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that external agencies have signed to comply with Sec 1798.90.52(b) for accessing your agency's ALPR data. These documents are also often required under the "Releasing ALPR Data" subsection of a California law enforcement agency's SB 34 policy.
A6. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that your agency has signed pursuant to Sec 1798.90.52(b) to access other agencies' ALPR data.
A7. All "records of access" required by Sec. 1798.90.52(a) for the periods June 5th, 2024 until the date of processing this request.
Items A5-A7 are not necessary if your agency provides the information requested in items A1-A4. We feel that items A1-A4 are more focused and would require less labor from your agency to produce.
Part B - Information Related to Numbers of “Detections” (plate scans) and “Hits” (plate scans that matched to a hotlist)
B1. The aggregate number of "detections" collected during 2025.
B2. The aggregate number of “hits” during 2025.
B3. The aggregate number of "detections" from June 5th, 2024 until the date this request is processed.  If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
B4. The aggregate number of “hits” from June 5th, 2024 until the date this request is processed. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. 
B5. Average Daily Scans based on the past amount of days in which you retain data.
The information requested in items B1-B4 is readily available through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Hit Ratio Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function. As noted above, this information exists within your system and can be easily exported with existing software.
Part C - Assistance, Description, and Recommendations.
We make the following requests under section 6253.1 of the California Public Records Act
C1. A description of the information technology and physical location in which the records exist.
C2. Assist us identifying records and information that will help the public understand how ALPR data and hotlists are shared, how much data is collected year-to-year, and how much of that data matches a hot list; and
C3. Provide suggestions for overcoming any practical basis for denying access to the information sought.
Please note that more than 80 California law enforcement agencies have provided the information requested in Parts A1-A4 and B1-B4 in previous years. For your reference we have included a PDF below with the names of the agencies that have historically provided these records.
(This URL links to a dataset with links to the records: https://www.eff.org/document/data-driven-2-california-dragnet-data-set)
Part D - Information Related to New Changes
D1. Flock Safety has announced that they are building a product called Flock Nova, which combines information from agencies into one place, such as “Unify CAD, RMS, video, LPR, and public records.” It is mentioned in this article (https://www.404media.co/license-plate-reader-company-flock-is-building-a-massive-people-lookup-tool-leak-shows/) that the tool is being used by some law enforcement agencies in an early access program (https://www.flocksafety.com/devices/flock-nova#:~:text=Flock%20Nova%20Early%20Access%20Now%20Open). Is/was your Agency part of, invited to, or booked a demo for the early access program? Is/has your Agency utilized Flock Nova? If yes, have you used the tool in any police investigations?
D2. Please send a document of the most up-to-date ALPR policy that your Agency uses.
Part E – Information Related to ALPR Camera Locations
E1. The location (address, intersection, coordinates, or nearest landmark) of each ALPR camera operated, maintained, or accessed by the Agency as of the date of this request.
E2. If your agency uses mobile ALPR cameras (e.g., on patrol cars), please indicate how many mobile units are currently deployed, and describe any policies that govern how locations of scans from mobile units are logged or stored.
E3. If exact addresses or coordinates are withheld, please provide general placement descriptions (e.g., "fixed camera at Main St & 2nd Ave," or "mobile ALPR unit assigned to Northern District patrol vehicle").
This information is typically accessible within ALPR system management software. For example:
- In Flock Safety camera location information is visible through the camera dashboard and deployment maps.
- In Vigilant/Motorola, camera locations are often listed in the device management or configuration settings.
Part F - ALPR Vendor Contracts and Purchase Agreements
F1. All contracts, purchase orders, service agreements, statements of work, or memoranda of understanding between the Agency and any vendor of ALPR technology (including Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, etc.) in effect during January 1, 2024–June 5, 2025. 
F2. All invoices, receipts, and payment records related to the purchase, installation, operation, or maintenance of ALPR systems during January 1, 2024–June 5, 2025. 
F3. Any grant applications or grant award documents (e.g., from DHS, DOJ, or private foundations) that funded or supported the acquisition or expansion of ALPR systems.
Part G – Internal Communications and Rationale for Placement
G1. Any internal memos, emails, reports, or presentations from 2022–2025 discussing where ALPR cameras should be placed or were ultimately placed. This includes communications from officers, command staff, city officials, or vendor representatives regarding:
- Placement location recommendations
- Crime statistics or justifications used to determine placement
- Community concerns or opposition
- Equity or privacy impacts
- “High crime” area designations or related targeting rationale
G2. Any communications with Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions, or other ALPR vendors from 2022–2025 regarding camera placement, pilot programs, early access programs, or system expansion.
CPRA requires you to undertake reasonable efforts to locate responsive records and to work in good faith with requesters to respond to their request. See CYAC v. City of National City, 220 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1430 (2013). Thus under the CPRA you are obligated to conduct a reasonable search and cannot deny a request merely because it might generate a large volume of records.
We ask that you please respond to this request within 10 days either by providing all the requested records or by providing a written response setting forth the legal authority on which you rely in withholding or redacting any document, as well as stating when documents will be made available. Should you choose to withhold or reject this request, we ask that you provide an explanation of why your agency is responding differently compared to the many others that have complied with our request.
Part H – ALPR Audit Logs & Access Records (More Detail)
H1. A list of all ALPR database users employed by the Agency between June 5, 2024 and the present, including job titles, divisions, and access levels. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. 
H2. Audit logs showing ALPR database access by Agency personnel from June 5, 2024 to the present. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period.  Please include:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the date above, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.
- Date and time of access
- Name and Job Title of User
- Search parameters (e.g., plate number or geographic query)
- Stated reason for access, if logged
2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period from the date above, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.
These logs are required under California Civil Code § 1798.90.52(a) and can help determine how often ALPR data is being queried and by whom. These records have been disclosed by several agencies in prior CPRA requests.
If the records responsive to any part of this request are held by another department, division, or city office (such as the Mayor’s Office, Department of Technology, SFMTA, or a surveillance oversight board), please identify the appropriate custodian or refer this request accordingly, as required under Government Code § 6253.1.
In particular, if your agency does not maintain records related to:
- The policy rationale or decision-making process for camera placement
- Contracts or procurement related to ALPR vendors
- Surveillance technology policy
Please inform me which department or office may hold these records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Omar Solis

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R016985-060825
communications.communication:
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK 
 
Dear Omar Solis: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of South San Francisco. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was created on 6/8/2025 and given the reference number R016985-060825 for tracking purposes. 
 Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

We request the following records related to Automated License Plate Readers ("ALPRs") and your law enforcement agency ("Agency").
Part A - Information related to ALPR data sharing.
A1. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shared ALPR data in 2025;
A2. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency received ALPR data in 2025;
A3. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information in 2025;
A4. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information in 2025.
The information requested in items A1-A4 is readily available for extraction through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Data Sharing Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function.
Most agencies have encountered little difficulty in providing this information (see below). In a small number of cases, an agency has interpreted items A1-A4 as requesting the creation of a record that does not exist. We disagree: this data is maintained by your software, can be exported through software your agency has already purchased, and is therefore subject to CPRA.
However, should you disagree, you may alternatively produce the following records:
A5. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that external agencies have signed to comply with Sec 1798.90.52(b) for accessing your agency's ALPR data. These documents are also often required under the "Releasing ALPR Data" subsection of a California law enforcement agency's SB 34 policy.
A6. All agreements, memoranda of understanding, requests, or other certifying documents that your agency has signed pursuant to Sec 1798.90.52(b) to access other agencies' ALPR data.
A7. All "records of access" required by Sec. 1798.90.52(a) for the periods June 5th, 2024 until the date of processing this request.
Items A5-A7 are not necessary if your agency provides the information requested in items A1-A4. We feel that items A1-A4 are more focused and would require less labor from your agency to produce.
Part B - Information Related to Numbers of “Detections” (plate scans) and “Hits” (plate scans that matched to a hotlist)
B1. The aggregate number of "detections" collected during 2025.
B2. The aggregate number of “hits” during 2025.
B3. The aggregate number of "detections" from June 5th, 2024 until the date this request is processed. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
B4. The aggregate number of “hits” from June 5th, 2024 until the date this request is processed. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period.
B5. Average Daily Scans based on the past amount of days in which you retain data.
The information requested in items B1-B4 is readily available through the ALPR software you use. For example, with Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, this data can be exported easily using the Hit Ratio Report function. With Flock Safety, this information is available through the transparency portal function. As noted above, this information exists within your system and can be easily exported with existing software.
Part C - Assistance, Description, and Recommendations.
We make the following requests under section 6253.1 of the California Public Records Act
C1. A description of the information technology and physical location in which the records exist.
C2. Assist us identifying records and information that will help the public understand how ALPR data and hotlists are shared, how much data is collected year-to-year, and how much of that data matches a hot list; and
C3. Provide suggestions for overcoming any practical basis for denying access to the information sought.
Please note that more than 80 California law enforcement agencies have provided the information requested in Parts A1-A4 and B1-B4 in previous years. For your reference we have included a PDF below with the names of the agencies that have historically provided these records.
(This URL links to a dataset with links to the records: https://www.eff.org/document/data-driven-2-california-dragnet-data-set)
Part D - Information Related to New Changes
D1. Flock Safety has announced that they are building a product called Flock Nova, which combines information from agencies into one place, such as “Unify CAD, RMS, video, LPR, and public records.” It is mentioned in this article (https://www.404media.co/license-plate-reader-company-flock-is-building-a-massive-people-lookup-tool-leak-shows/) that the tool is being used by some law enforcement agencies in an early access program (https://www.flocksafety.com/devices/flock-nova#:~:text=Flock%20Nova%20Early%20Access%20Now%20Open). Is/was your Agency part of, invited to, or booked a demo for the early access program? Is/has your Agency utilized Flock Nova? If yes, have you used the tool in any police investigations?
D2. Please send a document of the most up-to-date ALPR policy that your Agency uses.
Part E – Information Related to ALPR Camera Locations
E1. The location (address, intersection, coordinates, or nearest landmark) of each ALPR camera operated, maintained, or accessed by the Agency as of the date of this request.
E2. If your agency uses mobile ALPR cameras (e.g., on patrol cars), please indicate how many mobile units are currently deployed, and describe any policies that govern how locations of scans from mobile units are logged or stored.
E3. If exact addresses or coordinates are withheld, please provide general placement descriptions (e.g., "fixed camera at Main St & 2nd Ave," or "mobile ALPR unit assigned to Northern District patrol vehicle").
This information is typically accessible within ALPR system management software. For example:
- In Flock Safety camera location information is visible through the camera dashboard and deployment maps.
- In Vigilant/Motorola, camera locations are often listed in the device management or configuration settings.
Part F - ALPR Vendor Contracts and Purchase Agreements
F1. All contracts, purchase orders, service agreements, statements of work, or memoranda of understanding between the Agency and any vendor of ALPR technology (including Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, etc.) in effect during January 1, 2024–June 5, 2025.
F2. All invoices, receipts, and payment records related to the purchase, installation, operation, or maintenance of ALPR systems during January 1, 2024–June 5, 2025.
F3. Any grant applications or grant award documents (e.g., from DHS, DOJ, or private foundations) that funded or supported the acquisition or expansion of ALPR systems.
Part G – Internal Communications and Rationale for Placement
G1. Any internal memos, emails, reports, or presentations from 2022–2025 discussing where ALPR cameras should be placed or were ultimately placed. This includes communications from officers, command staff, city officials, or vendor representatives regarding:
- Placement location recommendations
- Crime statistics or justifications used to determine placement
- Community concerns or opposition
- Equity or privacy impacts
- “High crime” area designations or related targeting rationale
G2. Any communications with Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions, or other ALPR vendors from 2022–2025 regarding camera placement, pilot programs, early access programs, or system expansion.
CPRA requires you to undertake reasonable efforts to locate responsive records and to work in good faith with requesters to respond to their request. See CYAC v. City of National City, 220 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1430 (2013). Thus under the CPRA you are obligated to conduct a reasonable search and cannot deny a request merely because it might generate a large volume of records.
We ask that you please respond to this request within 10 days either by providing all the requested records or by providing a written response setting forth the legal authority on which you rely in withholding or redacting any document, as well as stating when documents will be made available. Should you choose to withhold or reject this request, we ask that you provide an explanation of why your agency is responding differently compared to the many others that have complied with our request.
Part H – ALPR Audit Logs & Access Records (More Detail)
H1. A list of all ALPR database users employed by the Agency between June 5, 2024 and the present, including job titles, divisions, and access levels. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period.
H2. Audit logs showing ALPR database access by Agency personnel from June 5, 2024 to the present. If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. Please include:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the date above, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.
- Date and time of access
- Name and Job Title of User
- Search parameters (e.g., plate number or geographic query)
- Stated reason for access, if logged
2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period from the date above, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.
These logs are required under California Civil Code § 1798.90.52(a) and can help determine how often ALPR data is being queried and by whom. These records have been disclosed by several agencies in prior CPRA requests.
If the records responsive to any part of this request are held by another department, division, or city office (such as the Mayor’s Office, Department of Technology, SFMTA, or a surveillance oversight board), please identify the appropriate custodian or refer this request accordingly, as required under Government Code § 6253.1.
In particular, if your agency does not maintain records related to:
- The policy rationale or decision-making process for camera placement
- Contracts or procurement related to ALPR vendors
- Surveillance technology policy
Please inform me which department or office may hold these records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Omar Solis

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fsouth-san-francisco-police-department-6782%252Fautomated-license-plate-reader-alpr-records-request-south-san-francisco-police-department-187606%252F%253Femail%253Dweb-pd%252540ssf.net&url_auth_token=AAAboeM7olx7Oihccb4rhwuhbBw%3A1uOTaN%3ApgsBJBb942c5RK_dGTVfVQYXdkzWbDwDILua1VPAzMU 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.  Please use the portal to respond to emails, ask questions, and follow the progress of your request. 
 
Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
 
Office of the City Clerk 
City of South San Francisco City Hall: 400 Grand Avenue • South San Francisco, CA 94080 • P.O Box 711 • South San Francisco, CA 94083 
Phone: 650-877-8518 • Fax 650-829-6641 • www.ssf.net

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R016985-060825
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

  OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK  OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK 
04/06/2026 RE: Public Records Request - Reference # R016985-060825 

Dear Omar , 

The City of South San Francisco has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Please log in to the Public Records Center to retrieve the appropriate responsive documents. 
Public Records Request - R016985-060825 This letter responds to your June 8, 2025 request submitted with the City of South San Francisco (“City”) for certain documents pursuant to the California Public Records Act (“CPRA”), Government Code sections 7920.005 et seq. The CPRA’s fundamental precept is that governmental records shall be disclosed to the public, upon request, unless there is a legal basis not to do so. The right of access to public records under the CPRA is not unlimited; it does not extend to records that are exempt from disclosure. 

To the extent that the City has records responsive to your request, and not exempt from disclosure under the CPRA, the City is providing such records to you. In addition, the City would like to make you aware that certain requested records are already publicly available, please review the City’s Flock safety transparency portal at the following link: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/south-san-francisco-ca-pd for information on the following parts of your request: A1, A3, A7, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, C1, C2, D2, H2 

Further, the City can inform you with regards to parts A2 and A4 of your request that the City receives ALPR data and hot list information from agencies in San Mateo County and the San Francisco Police Department. The City has no responsive records with regard to parts D1, E2, F3, G1, G2 of your request. 

The City has determined that certain information contained in records responsive to your request is exempt from disclosure under the CPRA. Exempt records have been redacted. The exemptions applicable to your request are as follows: 

The public interest exemption contained in Government Code section 7922.000 provides that records are exempt from disclosure if on the facts of the particular case the public interest served by not disclosing the record clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure of the record. Here, the requested records contain the serial numbers of the City’s ALPR cameras. The public interest in nondisclosure of the serial numbers clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure as nondisclosure protects the security and integrity of the City’s ALPR system and disclosure of the information does not promote transparency. In addition, the requested records contain certain financial information of the City. The public interest in nondisclosure of the City’s financial information clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure as nondisclosure protects the security and integrity of the City’s financial systems. Therefore, the serial numbers and financial information have been redacted in accordance with Government Code section 7922.000. 

The foregoing determination is made in consultation with the City Attorney. If you have any questions, please contact me. 

Sincerely, 
Jazmine Miranda Assistant City Clerk City Hall:  400 Grand Avenue • South San Francisco, CA 94080 • P.O. Box 711 •  South San Francisco, CA 94083 Phone: 650-877-8518 • Fax: 650-829-6641 • www.ssf.net

title: Todd Mullis case

communications.subject: Iowa Open Records Law Request: Todd Mullis case
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All material relating to the police investigation into Todd Michael Mullis, Agency Case Number 18010035.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mikita Brottman

title: AER - WA Dept of Health

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: AER - WA Dept of Health
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse medical event records filed with the Washington Department of Health between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Nolan

Please contact me within 30 days 5/6/2026 9:16:32 AM by e-mail at publicdisclosure@doh.wa.gov or by postal mail at: Public Records Officer, Washington State Department of Health, P.O. Box 47808, Olympia, WA 98504-7808, if you have any questions. NOTE:  Pop-up Blockers on your web browser will need to be disabled to download files from this site. 
Some information within your requested records may have been withheld. Please see the Redaction Summary located at the end of each document for a list of redactions that were applied. If any records were withheld in their entirety, please refer to the Exemption Log provided to you. 

Under RCW 42.56.520 you may appeal the decision to withhold information contained in the records via a request for review by the Department of Health’s Public Records Officer. When filing an appeal for Public Records, please include your Public Records Request reference number so the correct request can be reviewed. The request must be submitted in writing by one of the following methods: 

1.  Send an email request to PRRappeals@doh.wa.gov (mailto:PRRappeals@doh.wa.gov) 
OR 
2.  Mail your request to: 
Public Records Officer  
Washington State Department of Health 
P.O. Box 47808

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Daily snapshots of the Motor Carrier Census file (also known as Company Census or MCS-150 data) for the period January 1, 2000  through January 27, 2026.   If daily snapshots are not available, I would be interested in monthly or quarterly snapshots.  

Fields of interest (standard census fields):                                                                                                       
  - DOT_NUMBER                                                                                                                                       
  - LEGAL_NAME, DBA_NAME                                                                                                                             
  - PHY_STREET, PHY_CITY, PHY_STATE, PHY_ZIP                                                                                                         
  - NBR_POWER_UNIT (power units)                                                                                                                     
  - DRIVER_TOTAL (drivers)                                                                                                                           
  - MCS150_MILEAGE, MCS150_MILEAGE_YEAR                                                                                                              
  - CARRIER_OPERATION (interstate/intrastate)                                                                                                        
  - CARGO types (CRGO_* fields)                                                                                                                      
  - Authority status fields                                                                                                                          
  - MCS150_DATE (filing date)                                                                                                                        

Preferred format: CSV, pipe-delimited text, or any machine-readable format.                                                                        

Please note that this request has been made numerous times per a review of FOIA logs, specifically FMCS-2023-05358, FMCS-2023-03393, FMCS-2023-03511 in FY2023, and also in previous years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

communications.subject: FW: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Over the past year, FMCSA has improved the sharing of data with the public through the DOT - Public Data Portal<https://data.transportation.gov/> (Datahub).  By selecting "Trucking & Motorcoaches" you can search our many datasets.  You can use the search catalog in the upper right corner to type in key words like "Carrier" to see what kind of data we share with the public.   You are also able to search Company Census File which contains data for active, inactive an pending entities registered with FMCSA.

The FOIA provides that records made available under the FOIA's proactive disclosure provisions of subsection (a)(1) and (a)(2) are not themselves subject to individualized FOIA requests, see 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A). This information is already made publicly available through the FMCSA Datahub.

After reviewing the data we share with the public, please let us know if there is something you are unable to find.

Thank you
FMCSA FOIA Office

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Hi Jennifer

Thank you for your response.  Thank you for providing the two census snapshot files (CENSUS_FILE_231229 and CENSUS_FILE_20241227). We reviewed both files against the publicly available SODA Company Census File (data.transportation.gov, dataset az4n-8mr2) and found four issues that prevent us from using  the snapshots as delivered.

- Issue 1: Power unit counts are classification codes, not integers. The TOT_PWR field contains letter codes (A through Z) for 54% of rows  instead of numeric power unit counts. The SODA Company Census File provides clean integer values for the same carriers in the  NBR_POWER_UNIT field. Large carriers such as UPS (DOT 21800) and Walmart (DOT 63585) return empty or coded values in the delivered files  but show 28,307 and 13,618 power units respectively in the SODA table. We need the actual integer count.

- Issue 2: Mileage year is a date string, not a year. The MLG150_YEAR field contains date-formatted strings (eg "01-JUN-74") for 100% of  rows. The SODA table provides this as a 4-digit year integer (eg 2023). We need a 4-digit year matching the SODA field  MCS150_MILEAGE_YEAR.

- Issue 3: Mileage is missing for 17% of rows. The MLG150 field is empty for carriers that have populated mileage values in the SODA table,  including large national carriers. We need MLG150 populated for all carriers that have mileage in the SODA sms_input_census table.

- Issue 4: Header row truncated at 1024 bytes. The tab-delimited header is cut at exactly 1024 bytes, splitting the column name "TRMHMTRUCK"  across two lines. The file has 163 data columns but the header shows 93 on the first line and 71 on the second. We need the header  exported with no line-length limit.

- December 2025 snapshot. Your office indicated that a December 2025 snapshot is available on the DOT DataHub. We checked and this is not  the case. The Company Census File on DataHub (dataset az4n-8mr2) is a live, daily-updating dataset that reflects the current state of the  census as of today. It is not a historical snapshot archive. There is no December 2025 point-in-time file available on that platform.

The delivered files do contain usable data for DOT numbers, legal names, states, driver counts and operating status. The power unit and  mileage fields are the two we cannot use in their current format.

Thank you again for your diligence and support, I really appreciate it.

title: Lewis County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Lewis County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12, 2026

110 Center Avenue
Weston, WV 26452

To whom it may concern,

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Lewis County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.

Sincerely, Drew Solt
740-243-6793
drewsolt@yahoo.com

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Lewis County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following West Virginia Freedom of Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on March 20, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: FW: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Lewis County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Lewis County is a small county and we do no have a lot of activity to report on opioid funding- since the last request.  From March 2025 to date we received $47,146.39.  We do not use application forms.  We used $8, 705.76 for LE equipment since March 2025.  Those requesting funds come before the County Commission to do so and a vote occurs. I do not have any other forms to offer or submit towards your request.



Thank you and have a wonderful day,

Tina J. Helmick, MSW
Lewis County Commission
Administrator

title: Police Schedule Request

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Police Schedule Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A CSV containing any available officer identifying information (first name, last name, badge number/IDNO6, rank), geographic assignments (beats, districts, etc), dates, and shift time (clock in and clock out time) assigned to all police officers who are employed by the Boston Police Department. I would like these records to include the timeframe the Jan 1, 2021 through the most recent date available.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Vijay Fisch

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: B002707-091624
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Reference No: B002707-091624 Dear Vijay Fisch: The Boston Police Department (BPD) has received your request for public records.  This response applies only to records that exist and are in the custody of the BPD. See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, p. 32, n.115.  It is expected that a custodian of records must use her superior knowledge of her records with respect to responses to public records requests.  950 CMR 32.04(5).  Specifically, you stated: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

A CSV containing any available officer identifying information (first name, last name, badge number/IDNO6, rank), geographic assignments (beats, districts, etc), dates, and shift time (clock in and clock out time) assigned to all police officers who are employed by the Boston Police Department. I would like these records to include the timeframe the Jan 1, 2021 through the most recent date available. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Vijay Fisch 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fboston-police-department-174%252Fpolice-schedule-request-173397%252F%253Femail%253Dshawn.williams%252540boston.gov&url_auth_token=AAAj3fcYLuG3tA1ygoUNI_ZMLII%3A1sqOn5%3ANht7T1TLsWj15MDlcciozwRybj6RW9mQTZ_luFs20iI A response to a public records request must be provided within ten (10) business days from the business day a written request was received.  G. L. c. 66, § 10 (a); 950 CMR 32.06(2)(b).  The public records law permits a response time of up to twenty-five (25) business days from the business day a written request is received, so long as a detailed explanation is provided.  G. L. c. 66, § 10(b)(vi); 950 CMR 32.06(2)(i). Due to the nature of your request additional time is needed.  As such, BPD will need up to, but no more than, fifteen (15) additional business days to review and process your request.

title: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Skagit County Sheriff (washington))

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Skagit County Sheriff (washington))
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from January 1, 2010 to the date this request was filed, please provide a spreadsheet of all complaints against officers in CSV or Excel format with only the following columns: Investigation/Complaint No., Incident Type, Occurred Date, Complaint Reported On Date, Employee Badge Number, Employee First Name, Employee Last Name, Allegation, Allegation: Policy/Directive Violated, Allegation: Finding, Disposition, Discipline Imposed.

Please provide all complaints, regardless of severity,in cluding any complaints that may not have been classified for full investigation and were referred to an employee's supervisor for counseling or retraining as well as complaints that were investigated by an employee's command instead of the normal internal affairs process. I would ask that you fulfill my request in a single installment in one spreadsheet instead of splitting it up into multiple annual spreadsheets if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Your Skagit County, WA public records request #26-716 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
Skagit County, WA ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-716 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for submitting your request to Skagit County Records Department. Someone from our records team will respond as required by RCW 42.56.520, within five business days. ln calculating the five business days, the following are not counted: the day the county receives the request, Saturdays, Sundays and holidays. RCW 1.12.040. See also WAC 44-14-03006. lf the request is received by the County after County business hours. the request shall be deemed to have been received by the county on the next business day. A response may be (a) providing the records, (b) providing a link to records available online, (c) providing a reasonable estimated date of when the records will be available, (d) requesting clarification, or (e) denial of the request. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact me at 360-416-1152, or by email at pherman@co.skagit.wa.us. Sincerely, Pamela Herman Certified Public Records Officer, Central Services Skagit County 360-416-1152 Public Records Act (PRA) Notice. Any information, including certain personal information, submitted to the County by e-mail or other method may be subject to disclosure as a public record under RCW chapter 42.56.  ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Skagit County, WA.

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Santa Barbara Police Department public records request #26-216
communications.communication:
Santa Barbara Police Department ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-216: Per California Government Code §7922.535, agencies may take an additional 14 days to determine whether disclosable records exist for any of the following reasons: (1) The need to search for and collect the requested records from field facilities or other establishments that are separate from the office processing the request. (2) The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. (3) The need for consultation, which shall be conducted with all practicable speed, with another agency having substantial interest in the determination of the request or among two or more components of the agency having substantial subject matter interest therein. (4) The need to compile data, to write programming language or a computer program, or to construct a computer report to extract data. In order to determine whether disclosable records in our custody exist, the City requires the additional 14 days to respond as provided by the Government Code above and in compliance with that code, you will receive a response regarding your request no later than April 3, 2026. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Santa Barbara Police Department.

title: Request to Army for all reports on entertainment industry interaction/collaboration

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Army for all reports on entertainment industry interaction/collaboration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All Entertainment Liaison Office (ELO) reports, "Entertainment Media Weekly" reports, or other reports pertaining to interactions or collaborations between the US Army and entertainment industry entities, inclusive of all years for which records are still retained by the Army. I request a search of all locations and databases where such records may be found, including but not limited to public affairs offices.  

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

You have reached the Headquarters Department of Army Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Referral Center.

Our office is not the records owner of the documents that you are seeking. Requests for information and documents under the purview of the Army are referred to the proponent agency for appropriate handling in accordance with Army Regulation 25-55, The Army Freedom of Information Act program. Your FOIA request has been assigned a case tracking number: FA-25-1467 /FP-25-014197.

The correct office to process your request is Office of the Chief of Public Affairs. Your request was referred to that office via email for processing and direct response to you.

Note: Once the appropriate office receives your request, a new FOIA case tracking number will be assigned, and their contact information is listed below.

Office of the Chief of Public Affairs

Washington DC 20310

DA FOIA

communications.subject: [External Message Added] County of Napa public records request #26-245
communications.communication:
County of Napa ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-245: Napa County received your Public Records Act request. Govt. Code section 7922.535 provides in part as follows: “Each agency, upon a request for a copy of records, shall, within 10 days from receipt of the request, determine whether the request, in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records in the possession of the agency and shall promptly notify the person making the request of the determination and the reasons therefor ... In unusual circumstances, the time limit prescribed in this article ... may be extended by written notice … to the person making the request, setting forth the reasons for the extension and the date on which a determination is expected to be dispatched. No notice shall specify a date that would result in an extension for more than 14 days.” This response therefore serves as written notice of the need for an extension of time for your request. The reasons for the extension include * The need to search for and collect the requested records from field facilities or other establishments that are separate from the office processing the request. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(1).) * The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(2).) * The need for consultation, which shall be conducted with all practicable speed, with another agency having substantial interest in the determination of the request or among two or more components of the agency having substantial subject matter interest therein. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(3).) * The need to compile data, to write programming language or a computer program, or to construct a computer report to extract data. (Govt. Code section 7922.535(c)(4).) We have initiated the processing of your request to identify records and determine if any records are exempt from disclosure pursuant to Govt. Code section 7927.500. We aim to have a determination dispatched no later than 3/27/26, which will inform you of the anticipated date when any disclosable records will be made available to you, the copying charges if physical copies are desired, and the explanation for not disclosing any exempt records. Should you have any questions, please reply to this email. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at County of Napa.

Technical support: See our help page

County of Napa ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-245: A report with all data that is releasable is attached however names, DOB, and actual reports are not releasable because such information, taken as a whole, could comprise criminal history records. Criminal history records of an individual constitute local summary criminal history information per Penal Code Section 13300 et seq. and under the reasoning of Westbrook v. County of Los Angeles (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 157 and 89 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 204 (2006) and therefore are not subject to release under the Public Records Act. (See also Government Code Section 7927.705.) Additionally, no MOU outlined in section 5 exists. Thank you. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Livingston County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Livingston County)
communications.communication:
Dear County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request. 

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data. 

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion. 

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you. 
Alexander Munro 
Ypsilanti, MI 
734-272-6612 
awmunro@gmail.com

Your request will be forwarded to the relevant County department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. 
PLEASE NOTE: The Michigan Freedom of Information Act does not require a public body to make a compilation, summary or report of information except as provided in Section 11 of FOIA for a State Agency. The County is not required to provide records in a digital format or on digital media if the County does not already have technological capability do to do. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you will receive an email when your request has been completed.

title: Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to and copies of records related to water quality complaints and issues within the Albany, Schenectady, and Troy Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) for the period from January 1, 2018, to the present.

Specifically, I am seeking the following information:

1) The total number of water quality complaints received by your agency/authority/utility, broken down by year.

2) A detailed summary of each water quality complaint, including:
2a. The date of the complaint
2b. The location (address or nearest cross streets) of the complaint
2c. The nature of the complaint (e.g., odor, taste, discoloration, contamination concerns)
2d. Any actions taken by your agency/authority/utility to address the complaint
2e. The resolution or current status of the complaint

3) Any reports, memos, or other documentation related to water quality complaints, investigations, and resolutions within the specified area and timeframe.

If any of this information is available in electronic format, I kindly request that you provide it via email. If physical copies are necessary, please inform me of any copying or postage fees in advance, and I will make arrangements for payment.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a detailed explanation for the denial and inform me of any appeal procedures available under New York State law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jackson Parker

communications.communication:
Subject: FOIL Appeal for Denial of Access - Water Quality Complaints in Albany, Schenectady, and Troy MSA (Niskayuna Consolidated Water District)

Dear Records Access Officer,

I am writing to appeal the summary denial of access to records that I requested under the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) on March 15, 2023. The request sought records related to water quality complaints and issues within the Albany, Schenectady, and Troy Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) from January 1, 2018, to the present. I have not received a response, acknowledgment, or any communication from the Niskayuna Consolidated Water District regarding my request. Pursuant to FOIL, the agency is required to respond within five business days.

As a reminder, my original request sought the following information:

1) The total number of water quality complaints received by your agency/authority/utility, broken down by year.
2) A detailed summary of each water quality complaint, including the date, location, nature of the complaint, any actions taken, and the resolution or current status.
3) Any reports, memos, or other documentation related to water quality complaints, investigations, and resolutions within the specified area and timeframe.

I followed up on my original request with two subsequent emails on April 11, 2023, and April 26, 2023, requesting a response or an update on the status of my request. However, I have not received any acknowledgment or response from the Niskayuna Consolidated Water District.

The summary denial of access to the requested records is in violation of FOIL. I respectfully request that you reconsider my request and provide the requested records or a valid explanation for withholding them within ten business days, as required by FOIL.

If my appeal is denied, please provide a written explanation for the denial and inform me of any further appeal procedures available under New York State law.

Thank you for your attention to this matter, and I look forward to receiving your response.
